description_long: This is a large concrete skate ramp structure, commonly known as a half-pipe, designed for skateboarding, BMX, or similar activities. The ramp features high vertical walls on both sides, a flat central section, and is adorned with various graffiti, murals, and street art, giving it an urban, well-used appearance. Metal guardrails run along the top edges for safety, and there is a stairway on one end for access to the upper platform. The surface is smooth but shows signs of wear, with visible scuff marks and painted tags. The structure is open and accessible, allowing users to skate or walk along the curved surfaces and platforms. Its robust construction and size make it a prominent feature in any urban park or recreational area.
description: A large concrete half-pipe skate ramp with graffiti, metal guardrails, and a stairway for access, designed for urban recreational use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tall vertical wall, graffiti art, and a window-like opening with metal guardrails along the top.
description_view_1: Angled view into the ramp's interior, highlighting the curved skating surface, central flat area, and extensive graffiti.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with more graffiti, a mural, and the stairway structure visible on the right.
description_view_3: Top-down angled view showing the full width of the ramp, both curved sides, and the stairway access.
